data.title: Dmall Sets 2026 AGM to Approve Accounts, Board Slate and Share Issuance Mandate
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Dmall Inc. ( (HK:2586) ) has provided an update.

Dmall Inc. has called its annual general meeting for June 5, 2026, in Beijing, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2025, along with the directors’ and auditor’s reports. The meeting will also vote on the re-election of key board members, the renewal of KPMG as auditor, and a general mandate allowing directors to issue additional shares and related securities, a move that could provide funding flexibility but may dilute existing shareholders if exercised.

By seeking shareholder approval for a broad share issuance mandate, Dmall is aligning with common Hong Kong market practice to keep capital-raising options open for future growth initiatives or strategic investments. The planned reappointment of KPMG and re-election of existing directors signal continuity in governance and oversight, suggesting the company is prioritising stability while preserving strategic financial flexibility for its next phase of development.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2586) stock is a Buy with a HK$10.80 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Dmall Inc. stock, see the HK:2586 Stock Forecast page.

More about Dmall Inc.

Dmall Inc., incorporated in the British Virgin Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the digital retail and smart retail technology sector. The company provides digital solutions and services to supermarkets and retailers, focusing on enhancing operational efficiency and consumer experiences in the China market and potentially broader Asian retail ecosystems.

Average Trading Volume: 2,505,930

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$6.8B

data.title: Tesson Plans Discounted Share Placement Backed by Controlling Shareholder
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Tesson Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1201) ).

Tesson Holdings has agreed to place up to 70 million new shares at HK$1.50 each through Arta Asset Management on a best-efforts basis, representing about 18.94% of its existing share capital and raising net proceeds of roughly HK$103 million, subject to approval under a specific mandate. The deal, priced at a 33.63% discount to the benchmarked price, will be accompanied by an underwriting arrangement under which controlling shareholder Double Key will subscribe for up to 35 million unsubscribed shares, a connected transaction that triggers independent board review and shareholder approval but remains within Hong Kong listing rules on dilution.

The placement and underwriting structure are designed to secure funding while limiting theoretical dilution to about 14.63%, below the regulatory threshold, potentially strengthening the company’s capital base without breaching market protections. Governance safeguards include formation of an independent board committee and appointment of an independent financial adviser, with a circular to be sent to shareholders ahead of a special general meeting to vote on the mandate and connected transaction.

More about Tesson Holdings Ltd.

Tesson Holdings Ltd., incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates as an investment holding company with interests spanning various businesses. The company accesses capital markets through share placements and has a controlling shareholder, Double Key, that plays an active role in its financing arrangements and ownership structure.

The latest announcement is out from Lens Technology Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6613) ).

Lens Technology’s de facto controllers, Ms. Chau Kwan Fei and Mr. Cheng Chun Lung, plan to increase their indirect stake in the company’s A shares via their controlled entity, Changsha Qunxin Investment Consulting, which already holds 5.46% of the firm. Genfleet Therapeutics (Shanghai), Inc. Class H ( (HK:2595) ) has provided an announcement.

GenFleet Therapeutics has convened its 2025 annual general meeting for 11 May 2026 in Shanghai to seek shareholder approval on key corporate matters, including the 2025 board report, audited consolidated financial statements, annual report and directors’ remuneration for 2025 and 2026. Shareholders will also vote on reappointing Ernst & Young as auditor, revising rules for independent non-executive directors and related-party transaction management, and granting the board mandates to repurchase up to 10% of issued shares and to issue additional shares, moves that could influence capital structure and strengthen corporate governance.

These proposed resolutions, if passed, would renew the company’s financial reporting and audit arrangements, adjust oversight frameworks for independent directors and related-party dealings, and give management greater flexibility in executing share buy-backs and equity issuance. The package of approvals is likely to impact GenFleet’s capital management strategy, investor protections and governance practices, shaping its positioning in the Hong Kong market and its engagement with minority shareholders.

data.title: Modern Chinese Medicine Details Highly Concentrated Insider Shareholding
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Modern Chinese Medicine Group Co. Ltd. ( (HK:1643) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Modern Chinese Medicine Group Co., Ltd. has issued a supplemental announcement regarding a high concentration of its shareholding, clarifying the identities of key shareholders previously unnamed. The filing shows that Modern Biotechnology Group Holdings, wholly owned by chairlady and executive director Sun Xinlei, holds 55.8% of the company’s issued shares, while Universal Health (Global) Investment Fund Management, wholly owned by Chen Xiaoyan, holds 16.7%, with additional smaller stakes held by Chen’s spouse and son.

The disclosure highlights that a very large majority of the company’s shares are effectively controlled by insiders and related parties, underscoring limited free float and potential liquidity constraints for minority investors. The board states that, apart from these identified parties, it does not know the identities or individual holdings of the other unnamed shareholders referenced by regulators, providing additional transparency but also reinforcing the concentrated ownership structure.

More about Modern Chinese Medicine Group Co. Ltd.

Modern Chinese Medicine Group Co., Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed company in the healthcare sector, focusing on modernized traditional Chinese medicine products. The company is incorporated in the Cayman Islands and trades on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong under stock code 1643.

data.title: China Resources Gas Subsidiary Chongqing Gas Sees Profit Drop in 2025 but Maintains Dividends
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

China Resources Gas Group ( (HK:1193) ) has issued an announcement.

China Resources Gas Group has reported principal financial information for its subsidiary Chongqing Gas Group Corporation for the year ended 31 December 2025, showing relatively stable assets but weaker profitability. Chongqing Gas’s total assets slipped 0.39% to RMB11.03 billion, while net assets attributable to shareholders rose 1.82% to RMB5.80 billion, highlighting a modest strengthening of its balance sheet.

For 2025, Chongqing Gas posted a 1.41% increase in revenue to RMB10.31 billion, but net profit attributable to shareholders fell 45% to RMB210.58 million, with basic and diluted earnings per share down 48% to RMB0.13 and operating cash flow dropping 30.91%. Despite the earnings decline, the board has proposed a cash final dividend of RMB0.43 per 10 shares, following an interim dividend of RMB0.11, signaling a continued commitment to shareholder returns even as the company navigates margin pressure and lower returns on equity within China’s city-gas sector.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1193) stock is a Hold with a HK$19.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Resources Gas Group stock, see the HK:1193 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Resources Gas Group

China Resources Gas Group is a Bermuda-incorporated gas distribution company with significant operations in mainland China. Through its subsidiary Chongqing Gas Group Corporation, in which it holds an indirect 39.43% stake, the Group focuses on urban natural gas distribution and related services in Chongqing and surrounding markets, contributing to its broader city-gas portfolio across China.

data.title: RUSAL Pays Seventh Coupon on Yuan-Denominated Exchange-Traded Bonds
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

United Co RUSAL ( (HK:0486) ) has issued an update.

United Company RUSAL has disclosed a material fact notice confirming payment of coupon income on its BO-001P-15 series exchange-traded bonds, denominated in Chinese yuan but settled in Russian rubles under the issue terms. The bonds, with a nominal value of CNY 1,000 and a 540-day maturity, are part of the company’s broader 001P exchange-traded bond programme.

For the seventh coupon period from 18 March to 17 April 2026, RUSAL paid a total of RUB 124.944 million in interest to holders of 1.9 million bonds. The annual coupon rate is 7.25%, translating into RUB 65.76, or the equivalent of CNY 5.96 at the Bank of Russia exchange rate on 16 April 2026, per bond, underscoring the company’s continued servicing of its local bond obligations and its use of yuan-linked instruments within the Russian market framework.

More about United Co RUSAL

United Company RUSAL is an international public joint-stock company incorporated in Jersey and continued in the Russian Federation, with its securities listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, Moscow Exchange and SPB Exchange. The group is one of Russia’s major corporate issuers, active in the debt capital markets through exchange-traded bond programmes targeted at institutional and professional investors.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Lingbao Gold Group Company Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3330) ) just unveiled an update.

Lingbao Gold Group has clarified how it will use the roughly HK$770.6 million in net proceeds from its recent top-up subscription of new H shares, earmarking the funds primarily for overseas expansion of its gold mining business and general working capital. The announcement underscores the group’s push to strengthen its global resource base and support ongoing operations.

Around 65% of the proceeds, or about HK$500.9 million, will fund expansion works at St Barbara Mining’s Simberi gold mine in Papua New Guinea, which the company acquired control of in April 2026, with completion and operation targeted for late 2027. A further 5%, or roughly HK$38.5 million, is allocated to future overseas mergers and acquisitions of sizeable, cash-generative gold projects by the end of 2026, reinforcing Lingbao Gold’s capacity to move quickly on cross-border deals and deepen its overseas footprint despite no specific new targets yet identified.

data.title: Dynagreen Reshapes Board as Veteran Executive Hu Shengyong Becomes Employee Director
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1330) ) is now available.

Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group announced that long-serving executive Mr. Hu Shengyong has resigned as an executive director and member of the nomination committee, following adjustments to the company’s corporate governance structure, with both sides confirming there is no disagreement. At the same time, Hu was elected by the employee representative meeting as the board’s employee director for the current term, a move that preserves his influence within the group while strengthening employee representation and leaving the board to plan his reappointment to the nomination committee without additional director’s fees.

Hu, a senior accountant who has held multiple key roles including CFO, board secretary and executive director since 2008, will continue to serve as deputy Party Committee secretary, trade union chairman and now employee director. His transition from executive to employee director suggests a reshaping rather than a reduction of his role in governance, maintaining continuity for stakeholders while aligning the board’s composition with PRC company law and listed-company governance guidelines.

More about Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group Co., Ltd. Class H

Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group Co., Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated environmental services company listed in Hong Kong that focuses on environmental protection projects and related operations. The group’s activities are supported by a governance structure that includes executive, non-executive, independent and employee directors representing management, shareholders and staff interests.

data.title: YH Entertainment Deepens Shenzhen Yiqi Ties in Five-Year Pop Toy Deal
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from YH Entertainment Group ( (HK:2306) ).

YH Entertainment Group has formalised a long-term strategic cooperation between its subsidiaries Tianjin Yihua and Yuhua Tongxing and connected party Shenzhen Yiqi to develop and operate pop toy-related business. Under the agreements, effective from January 2025 to January 2030, Yuhua Tongxing will provide promotion services using the group’s managed artists, share economic benefits from sales of WAKUKU pop toys whose IP is owned by Shenzhen Yiqi, and sell pop toys based on its own IP to Shenzhen Yiqi and its affiliates.

In return, Yuhua Tongxing will pay Shenzhen Yiqi for consignment and marketing of pop toys, online marketing, IP design and production, and procurement of toys linked to Shenzhen Yiqi-owned or licensed IP, embedding Shenzhen Yiqi deeply in its value chain. As Yuhua Tongxing is no longer deemed an insignificant subsidiary, these arrangements now constitute continuing connected transactions under Hong Kong listing rules, triggering ongoing disclosure and annual review requirements and the appointment of an independent financial adviser to justify the five-year term as consistent with normal business practice.

More about YH Entertainment Group

YH Entertainment Group, listed in Hong Kong, operates through subsidiaries such as Tianjin Yihua and Yuhua Tongxing and is active in entertainment-related commercialisation, including artist management and licensing. The group is expanding into the pop toys segment, leveraging its managed artists and intellectual property to collaborate with consumer brands and IP owners in China.

data.title: Asia Television Holdings Revamps Company Secretary and Compliance Roles
data.description:

Asia Television Holdings Limited ( (HK:0707) ) has shared an update.

Asia Television Holdings Limited has announced a series of senior corporate governance changes involving its company secretary and authorised representatives, reflecting an ongoing reshuffle of key compliance roles. Former company secretary and authorised representative Ho Yuk Ming Hugo, as well as successor Yu Tak Shing Eric and authorised representative Tang Po Yi, have all ceased their respective roles over the period from August 2025 to March 2026.

The board has appointed executive director Zha Mengling as an authorised representative from 13 April 2026 and named veteran solicitor Ling Wing Shan as both company secretary and authorised representative effective 17 April 2026. Ling brings over two decades of experience in corporate and dispute resolution law, with multi-jurisdictional qualifications and a background in international law firms and a Fortune 100 corporation, a move likely aimed at strengthening the company’s legal, regulatory and listing compliance capabilities.

More about Asia Television Holdings Limited

Asia Television Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operating in the media and entertainment sector through its television-related businesses. The company is governed by a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors and is subject to the Listing Rules of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

data.title: Shenzhen Han’s CNC Updates 2025 Final Dividend Timetable and Tax Treatment
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Shenzhen Han’s CNC Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3200) ) is now available.

Shenzhen Han’s CNC Technology Co., Ltd. has announced an updated final dividend for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, declaring a cash payout of RMB 6 per 10 shares to its H shareholders. The update specifies the timetable for shareholder approval on 8 May 2026, an ex-dividend date of 13 May 2026, the book closure from 15 to 18 May 2026, and confirms that the dividend will be paid in Hong Kong dollars with the exact amount and payment date to be announced.

The company also detailed the withholding tax arrangements for different categories of non-resident and mainland investors holding H shares, including a 10% enterprise income tax for non-resident enterprises and a 10% base rate for non-resident individuals, subject to applicable tax treaties. Mainland individual investors and certain funds accessing the stock via Shanghai-Hong Kong and Shenzhen-Hong Kong Stock Connect will face a 20% withholding rate, clarifying after-tax entitlements and providing greater transparency for cross-border investors ahead of the distribution.

More about Shenzhen Han’s CNC Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Shenzhen Han’s CNC Technology Co., Ltd. is a China-based industrial company listed in Hong Kong, with its H shares traded under stock code 03200. The firm operates in the precision manufacturing and CNC equipment sector, serving industrial customers that rely on automated machining solutions in both mainland China and overseas markets.
